# name: # Canonical dependency manifest for TOKEN-FAIRNESS (scheduler). TAB-separated (4 fields).
# The deterministic core is a self-contained static binary (bin/tf) — zero runtime deps. The
# hooks and the session.json writer use jq; off-peak cron uses flock + the claude CLI. Everything
# degrades gracefully (a missing tool narrows a capability, never a false "safe").

install-hint: preinstalled (the hook shim hooks/tf-hook.sh is bash)

probe-command: test -x ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/bin/tf-$(uname -m)-$(uname -s | tr A-Z a-z) || command -v tf
install-hint: ships per-arch under bin/; build with `cargo build --release` or `cargo install`
apt install jq (session-tokens .tokens writer + startup-report; the binary itself needs no jq)
util-linux (single-instance guard for OS-cron off-peak runs; degrades to a bare lock)
the Claude Code CLI — only the headless off-peak runner (`tf run-offpeak`) needs it
cron (durable OS-level arming via `tf oscron`; in-session CronCreate works without it)
